keyPoints: 6-week randomized field study comparing Prompt-for-Challenge vs baseline prompts among professional writers.
Primary outcomes: originality/novelty (automated indices plus blinded expert originality ratings) and blinded quality ratings.
Blinding integrity, structured rubrics, and inter-rater reliability (ICC) as measurement safeguards.
Four core PFC affordances—trade-off analysis, mitigation generation, similar-by-schema, card-level Q&A—plus traceable prompt logs.
Comprehensive data capture of drafts, prompt interactions, version histories, iteration counts, and time-on-task.
Mixed-effects analysis plan with writer and week as random effects and condition as fixed effect, plus robustness checks and trajectory insights.
Interpretation scaffolds for potential outcomes to guide design implications and post-AI workflow recommendations.
